value_target.status_rationale: Treat the target as not yet improving until the customer-owned comparison window and aggregate work-pattern movement support a stronger readout.

vbd_context.summary: Velocity, Breadth, and Depth are aggregate work-pattern context; they guide intervention planning and do not prove business impact.

likely_blockers.label: AI Fluency is mixed
likely_blockers.rationale: The function may need more confidence, usage quality, or leadership reinforcement before workflow change scales.
likely_blockers.evidence_basis: Aggregate AI Fluency follow-up by function.

likely_blockers.label: Velocity is not increasing
likely_blockers.rationale: The workflow may not be showing enough aggregate AI-enabled activity to expect business metric movement yet.
likely_blockers.evidence_basis: Aggregate Velocity movement for the selected workflow.

likely_blockers.label: Breadth is still limited
likely_blockers.rationale: AI-enabled work may be concentrated in too few roles or workflow steps to affect the function-level metric.
likely_blockers.evidence_basis: Aggregate Breadth by function and workflow family.

likely_blockers.label: Depth is still shallow
likely_blockers.rationale: People may be trying AI, but the work pattern has not moved deeply enough into repeatable workflow behavior.
likely_blockers.evidence_basis: Aggregate Depth evidence for repeated, verified workflow use.

likely_blockers.label: Customer assumptions need review
likely_blockers.rationale: Staffing, rollout, process, or case-mix changes may explain metric movement and need owner review.
likely_blockers.evidence_basis: Customer-owned assumptions attached to the governed ROI scenario.

recommended_interventions.label: Run targeted workflow enablement
recommended_interventions.owner: AI program owner and workflow owner
recommended_interventions.action: Run a focused enablement cycle on the selected workflow, including examples, verification habits, and leadership reinforcement.
recommended_interventions.rationale: The fastest improvement path is to help the function use AI in the actual workflow, not just increase generic usage.

recommended_interventions.label: Redesign the AI handoff in the workflow
recommended_interventions.action: Review where AI should enter the workflow and remove steps that keep users returning to the old process.
recommended_interventions.rationale: If Velocity is not increasing, the workflow may need a clearer AI-enabled path before the value metric can move.

recommended_interventions.label: Expand the workflow playbook to more roles
recommended_interventions.action: Identify adjacent roles in the same function and extend the approved workflow playbook after the pilot group stabilizes.
recommended_interventions.rationale: Breadth must expand at the function level before organization-level value modeling becomes credible.

recommended_interventions.label: Strengthen the evidence review
recommended_interventions.action: Review baseline, comparison, source, and operating assumptions before stronger value language changes.
recommended_interventions.rationale: The right move may be better evidence, not a stronger claim.

retest_plan.measurement_plan: Recheck aggregate AI Fluency, Velocity, Breadth, Depth, and the selected functional metric after the intervention window.
retest_plan.success_signal: The function shows improving VBD movement and the customer-owned metric moves in the expected direction.
retest_plan.decision_after_retest: Expand, adjust the intervention, strengthen evidence, or hold value language based on aggregate results.

next_data_needed: Aggregate AI Fluency follow-up by function
Velocity, Breadth, and Depth movement by workflow family
Median resolution time from Support case management system
Customer-owned baseline and comparison window review
Customer-owned assumptions for operating changes during the same period

allowed_phrases: The value target is not improving yet, so the team should adjust the intervention and re-measure.
VBD movement can guide the next workflow intervention, but customer outcome data owns the value test.
This is an advisory improvement loop, not proof of ROI or causality.
required_caveats: All guidance is aggregate-only and should be reviewed with the customer owner.
VBD and AI Fluency guide intervention planning; they do not prove business impact.
Customer-owned outcome evidence is required before stronger value language.
